About: It is about aspects of tribal Kurumba life in the Nilgiri Mountains.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with prehistoric and Neolithic rock art.
It is associated with the local Kurumba community.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the Neolithic period.
ConsistsOf: It consists of depictions of animals and human figures.
CreatedAround: It was created around 3,000 years ago.
CreatedIn: It was created in the Nilgiri Mountains.
It was created in the Nilgiri Hills of Tamil Nadu.
DiscoveredAt: It was discovered at the Jackanarai and Eluthu Paarai archaeological sites.
It was discovered at Eluthu Paarai.
DiscoveredBy: It was discovered by American anthropologist Allen Zackerel.
DiscoveredIn: It was discovered in the Nilgiris district.
HasA: It has a continuity on dwelling structures in the Nilgiris.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by the exclusive use of natural pigments sourced from the local environment.
It is characterized by themes rooted in the community’s everyday life, cultural practices, and close interaction with the natural environment of the Nilgiris.

It is characterized by imagery commonly including scenes of forests and wildlife, traditional dwellings constructed from leaves and wood, women engaged in drying food grains, and men involved in honey gathering.
It is characterized by social and ceremonial aspects of Kurumba life such as weddings and ritual observances.
It is characterized by elements of domestic livelihood including earthen pens for rearing hens.
It is characterized by portrayals of wild animals moving through forested landscapes.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by ancient rock art.
IsA: It is a prehistoric art form.
KnownAs: It is known as Kurumbar painting.
KnownFor: It is known for its continuity on dwelling structures in the Nilgiris.
It is known for depicting animals and human figures.
It is known for its abstract red-ochre paintings.
It is known for its distinctive aesthetic.
It is known for demonstrating indigenous knowledge of local plant resources and sustainable material practices.
It is known for its cultural authenticity and ecological significance.
It is known for reflecting the ecological, social, and cultural worldview of the Kurumba people.
LocatedIn: It is located in the Nilgiri Mountains.
It is located in modern-day Tamil Nadu, India.
It is located in the Nilgiris.
It is located in the Nilgiri Hills of Tamil Nadu.
It is located in the Nilgiri district.
It is located in the Kothagiri region.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the Nilgiri Mountains.
It occurred in modern-day Tamil Nadu, India.
It occurred in the Nilgiri Hills of Tamil Nadu.
It occurred in the Nilgiri district.
It occurred in the Kothagiri region.
PartOf: It is part of the Neolithic period.
UsedFor: It is used for depicting aspects of tribal Kurumba life.
UsedIn: It is used in dwelling structures.
UsedTo: It is used to demonstrate indigenous knowledge of local plant resources and sustainable material practices.
